classification_rationale: Pure TypeScript math primitives library with zero deps and no I/O: README.md:5 "a pure TypeScript library providing deterministic mathematical functions for time value of money, curve primitives, valuation models, and signal decay"; AGENTS.md:74 "Depends on: NONE (pure math library...)". It is consumed by reactor/core/mint/token (AGENTS.md:73) but defines no protocol schemas, dialects, or canonical type contracts. It is a shared utility (SUPPORTING) underpinning deterministic transforms rather than a normative spec or the reference spine itself. The one borderline element is src/emissions/emissionsSchedule.ts which calls itself "Canonical implementation" of the emissions model, but it is an off-chain numeric helper, not the on-chain authority, so it does not raise the whole repo to NORMATIVE.
primary_purpose: Pure, deterministic, dependency-free TypeScript library of financial/scoring math primitives (time value of money, curve shaping, reverse DCF/implied-rate, signal decay/half-life, and a three-phase emissions schedule) shared across AFI Protocol repos for scoring, tokenomics, and pipeline computation.
protocol_touchpoints: replay/determinism (pure deterministic transforms underpinning reproducible scoring)
DAG/pipeline (curve/decay transforms consumed by reactor scoring)
USS ingest (decay/greeks field mapping, docs-only reference)

reference_only_assumptions.quote: This library is Phase 1 ready and serves as the canonical math reference for the AFI ecosystem.
Canonical implementation of the three-phase front-loaded emissions model. Derived from AFI_Emissions_Final.wl (Wolfram Mathematica)
5. afi-token: Smart contracts and on-chain tokenomics ... This separation ensures that: Math functions can be audited independently

contradictions.title: Emissions cap/curve hardcoded despite 'no policy constants' rule
contradictions.detail: AGENTS.md forbids embedding AFI-specific policy constants and instructs passing them as arguments, but emissionsSchedule.ts ships DEFAULT_EMISSIONS_PARAMS with a hardcoded 86B cap, phase years, milestone fractions (1/3, 0.8, 1.0) and shape factors. These ARE policy/tokenomics constants. Mitigant: params are overridable via Partial input, so the constants are defaults not enforced values. Still a code/doc contradiction and a tokenomics-source ambiguity vs the on-chain emissions authority.
contradictions.evidence: AGENTS.md:92 'No hardcoded policy constants: AFI-specific parameters (e.g., decay rates, discount rates) should be passed as arguments, not embedded in functions.' vs src/emissions/emissionsSchedule.ts:59-73 'export const DEFAULT_EMISSIONS_PARAMS ... cap: 86_000_000_000n ... shapeEarly: 2.0'

contradictions.title: Off-chain emissions schedule presented as 'canonical' vs on-chain emissions caps being the commitment-plane authority
contradictions.detail: Per the portable-protocol north star, emissions caps / epoch linkage belong to the on-chain commitment plane (afi-token/afi-mint). This repo labels its JS schedule 'Canonical implementation' and exposes getRemainingSupply/getCumulativeEmissions which read like supply-accounting logic. If consumers treat this off-chain TS as the source of truth for caps rather than the contracts, it collapses commitment-plane authority into a utility lib. Should be clarified as off-chain simulation/derivation of the on-chain schedule.
contradictions.evidence: src/emissions/emissionsSchedule.ts:215-217 'export function getRemainingSupply(schedule, alreadyMinted){ return Math.max(0, Number(schedule.params.cap) - alreadyMinted); }'; AGENTS.md:157 'afi-token may reference emissions curves from afi-math (for off-chain simulation)'

contradictions.title: Stale / inconsistent consumer repo names
contradictions.detail: .afi-codex.json lists consumers using older names (afi-engine, afi-token-finalized, afi-plugins) while AGENTS.md uses current names (afi-core, afi-mint, afi-token). README likewise references afi-token-finalized and afi-engine. Mixed naming across the same repo's own metadata can drive wrong dependency/architecture decisions.
contradictions.evidence: .afi-codex.json:21-28 'consumers: [afi-engine, afi-token-finalized, afi-config, afi-plugins, afi-reactor, afi-core]'; README.md:182-185 'afi-engine: Signal processing... afi-token-finalized: Smart contracts'; AGENTS.md:6 'used across afi-reactor, afi-core, afi-mint, and afi-token'

notable_findings.title: Library is genuinely pure/deterministic — strong replay-supporting property
notable_findings.evidence: AGENTS.md:81 'All functions are referentially transparent (same inputs -> same outputs)'; AGENTS.md:133 forbids 'non-deterministic functions (e.g., random number generation, Date.now())'; src/index.ts:12 'All functions are deterministic and side-effect free.'
notable_findings.recommendation: Keep this guarantee; these decay/curve/emissions transforms are exactly the kind of pinned, version-recorded transforms the VALIDATOR_REPLAY_SPEC depends on. If reactor/scoring pins plugin/analyst versions, the afi-math version (currently 0.1.0) should be among the pinned, replay-recorded versions so a different stack can reproduce scored outputs.

notable_findings.title: Emissions schedule module is the only protocol-policy-bearing code; floats used for token accounting
notable_findings.evidence: src/emissions/emissionsSchedule.ts:41 'Per-epoch emission amounts (in base units, not wei)' and emissions computed as JS number floats (line 144 'emissions = baseEmissions.map(e => e * scale)') while cap is bigint (line 60 'cap: 86_000_000_000n').
notable_findings.recommendation: Float emission amounts cannot be authoritative for on-chain token accounting (precision loss vs wei/bigint). Clarify in docs that this module is off-chain simulation/derivation only and that the on-chain afi-token contract is the canonical emissions cap/epoch authority. If outputs are ever anchored on-chain, define a deterministic float->integer (wei) quantization rule to preserve replay determinism.

notable_findings.title: USS/CPJ/vault/TSSD/mint-contract/replay artifacts entirely absent (correctly)
notable_findings.evidence: grep for mongodb|TSSD|VaultedSignalRecord|cpj|AFIMintCoordinator|AFISignalReceipt|EmissionsMinted|publicSurface|proprietaryDetail|kafka|postgres|timescaledb|snowflake|datalake returned NO hits in src/; USS appears only as a docs mapping example (docs/AFI_MATH_OVERVIEW.md:190-214).
notable_findings.recommendation: None. Confirms correct separation of concerns: this repo holds no canonical dialect, vault, ledger, or replay-manifest artifacts. No normative schema lives here.

notable_findings.title: USS decay/greeks field-to-function mapping documented but not enforced
notable_findings.evidence: docs/AFI_MATH_OVERVIEW.md:210-214 'telemetry.decay.halfLifeDays -> decay.exponentialDecay({ halfLife }) ... telemetry.greeks.thetaPerDay -> decay.greeksAdjustedHalfLife({ thetaPerDay })'
notable_findings.recommendation: This mapping is documentation only; the canonical USS schema lives in afi-config/schemas/usignal/v1/. Ensure the doc stays consistent with the actual USS v1.1 telemetry field names; treat afi-config schema as the source of truth, not this overview.

notable_findings.title: Doctrine references afi-reactor as THE DAG orchestrator (downstream, not normative here)
notable_findings.evidence: AGENTS.md:141 'afi-reactor (DAG orchestrator): afi-reactor consumes decay functions from afi-math'; docs/AFI_MATH_OVERVIEW.md:22 '4. afi-reactor: Pipeline orchestration and DAG execution'
notable_findings.recommendation: Observation only: afi-math correctly stays an unopinionated dependency (afi-reactor -> afi-math, never reverse, AGENTS.md:143-144). The reactor-as-orchestrator framing is a downstream-doc concern, not a coupling defect in this repo.

notable_findings.title: tsconfig/build emits dist/ but it is gitignored — audit dist hits are build artifacts
notable_findings.evidence: .gitignore lists 'dist/'; grep matches under ./dist/emissions/emissionsSchedule.d.ts are generated declaration files, not authored source.
notable_findings.recommendation: None. Disregard dist/* matches; only src/, docs/, *.md, package.json, .afi-codex.json are authored surface.
